author: Robert Carr
description: A creamy blended nonalcoholic pina colada with frozen pineapple, pineapple juice, sweetened cream of coconut, coconut milk, ice, and a tiny pinch of salt. It is sweet, smooth, and tropical without rum flavor.
ingredients: 2 cups cold pineapple juice, plus 1/4 cup more if needed
1 cup sweetened cream of coconut, stirred smooth
1 cup canned full-fat coconut milk, shaken well
instructions: Shake the can of coconut milk well before opening. Stir or shake the cream of coconut until it is completely smooth.
Add 2 cups cold pineapple juice, 1 cup cream of coconut, 1 cup coconut milk, and 1/8 teaspoon fine salt to a blender.
Add 3 cups frozen pineapple chunks and 2 cups ice.
Blend on high until completely smooth and creamy, stopping once to scrape down the sides if needed.
Check the texture. For a thicker frozen drink, blend in another 1/2 to 1 cup ice. For a looser, more sippable drink, blend in pineapple juice 2 tablespoons at a time.
Taste and adjust only if needed. For a sweeter drink, blend in 1 to 2 tablespoons more cream of coconut. For a brighter drink, add a tiny squeeze of fresh lime juice.
Pour into cold hurricane glasses or tall glasses and serve immediately.
notes: This recipe is fully nonalcoholic and does not use rum extract, bitters, or anything meant to imitate rum. It should taste like pineapple, coconut, and sunshine, not alcohol.
Use sweetened cream of coconut, such as Coco Lopez, not unsweetened coconut cream. Cream of coconut is what gives the drink its sweet, beach-bar pina colada flavor.
Frozen pineapple makes the drink thicker and more flavorful than using ice alone.
Canned full-fat coconut milk makes the drink creamy without adding dairy. Shake the can hard before opening so the coconut fat and liquid recombine.
The tiny pinch of salt should not taste salty. It makes the pineapple and coconut taste sweeter and fuller.
If making this for someone who prefers it very sweet, add the extra cream of coconut before adding more pineapple juice.
If the blender struggles, add a splash more pineapple juice and blend again.
title: Nonalcoholic Pina Colada
